title: Arthur Jeffrey Dempster
body: Arthur Jeffrey Dempster (August 14, 1886 – March 11, 1950) was a Canadian-American physicist best known for his work in mass spectrometry and his discovery in 1935 of the uranium isotope 235U. As part of his research, he investigated the isotopic constitution of multiple other elements.
categories: Canadian emigrants to the United States
Fellows of the American Physical Society
Members of the American Philosophical Society
Members of the United States National Academy of Sciences
Presidents of the American Physical Society
